prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: condensed Fukui functions f+ (electrophilic attack), f− (nucleophilic attack), f0 (radical attack), and dual descriptor per atom; identification of the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding method; no Gaussian basis set, no DFT functional).
Geometry provenance: fixed input coordinates supplied by user (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz”); single-point evaluation only (no geometry optimization) → notation GFN2-xTB//input-XYZ.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model requested).
Charge and multiplicity: charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet); cation/anion states were treated as doublets (default for N±1 from a singlet).
Software: xtb backend as run via chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill (exact CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 <input>). Version was not reported in the result JSON.
Convergence & warnings: SCF calculations for the neutral, cation, and anion states all converged (integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true). Result JSON contained no entries in its `warnings` array; no engine warnings were reported. Charge-conservation checks for Σf+ and Σf− both passed (ok=true, detail "sum(f±) = 1.0000").

Key numerical results (per-atom values in e):
• Most electrophilic site (largest f+):  Fe atom (index 11, 1-based)  f+ = 0.22885456
• Most nucleophilic site (largest f−):   Fe atom (index 11, 1-based)  f− = 0.25938191
• Dual descriptor on Fe (f+ − f−):      −0.030527354 (negative → Fe favors nucleophilic behavior over electrophilic)

The full set of 21-atom f+, f−, f0, and dual-descriptor arrays, the Mulliken charges of the N, N±1 states, and the automatically generated bar-chart PNG are available in the output JSON and plot file written by chemkit (see paths below).

Reproducibility artifacts:
• Output JSON (full results): /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpovt_8zkb/live.json
• Bar-chart PNG: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/argo_o3/20260704-190657_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
• Live engine log (.out) was streamed during execution to the working directory (path shown in terminal during run; available on request).

No experimental comparison is provided because the user requested only the computed Fukui reactivity indices.

Headline result (most electrophilic f+ on Fe): 0.22885456 e (GFN2-xTB, gas phase).
